title: Program to Print Full Pyramid Pattern (Star Pattern)
description:
Given an integer N, the task is to print a full pyramid pattern with N rows. In this pattern 
 each row contains an odd number of stars, ranging from 1 star in the first row to (2 * N - 1) stars in the Nth row /n All the stars are center-aligned.
output: A full pyramid pattern of stars with N rows.

Example.explanation: Each row increases stars by 2 and is centered.

title: Program to find if a character is vowel or Consonant
description: Given a character, determine if it is a vowel or a consonant.

title: Print Fibonacci Series
description: Print the Fibonacci series up to a given number of terms.
input: N: the number of terms (1 <= N <= 10)
output: The first N terms of the Fibonacci sequence separated by spaces.

Example.explanation: Start from 0 and 1, each next number is sum of previous two.

title: Floor in a Sorted Array
description: Find the floor of a given number in a sorted array.
input: N: size of array , array element , target number X
output: The floor of X in the array, or -1 if it does not exist.

Example.explanation: The floor of 5 is 2, which is the largest number less than or equal to 5 in the array.

title: Move All Zeroes to End
description: Move all zeroes to the end of the array while maintaining the relative order of non-zero elements.
output: Array with all zeroes moved to the end, preserving relative order of non-zero elements.

Example.explanation: All zeros are moved to the end while keeping other elements in order.

title: T-primes
description: Find all T-primes in a given range.

Example.explanation: T-primes are numbers that are squares of prime numbers. In the range 1 to 10, the T-primes are 4 (2^2) and 9 (3^2).

input: Range of numbers (start and end) , maximum number in the range 10
output: All T-primes within the specified range.

title: Happy Number
description: Determine if a number is a happy number.
output: true if N is a happy number, false otherwise.

title: Lucky Numbers in a Matrix
description: Find all lucky numbers in a given matrix.
input: A matrix in JSON array format (e.g. [[3,7,8],[9,11,13],[15,16,17]]).
output: The lucky number that is the minimum in its row and maximum in its column.

Example.explanation: 15 is smallest in its row and largest in its column.

title: Find the Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array of integers, every element appears twice except for one. Find that single one. Use O(1) extra space for an extra challenge.
input:
N: size of array (1 <= N <= 9)
arr: array elements separated by space
output: The element that appears only once.
